nodes.name: 📥 Webhook — Receive CSV Upload
nodes.notes: Entry point: accepts multipart/form-data POST with a CSV file attached as 'file' field. Supports manual uploads and can be triggered from any HTTP client (curl, Postman, etc).

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { "status": "received", "message": "Invoice file received and queued for processing", "timestamp": new Date().toISOString() } }}

nodes.name: ✅ Respond — Acknowledged
nodes.notes: Immediately acknowledge receipt to the caller so they don't timeout. Processing continues asynchronously.

nodes.name: 🔐 Hash File — SHA-256 + Extract
nodes.notes: Computes SHA-256 hash of the raw file bytes. Used for idempotency — if same file is uploaded twice, we can detect and skip reprocessing. Also extracts filename for audit.

nodes.name: 📋 Parse CSV — Map Fields + Fill Math
nodes.notes: Parses raw CSV text into structured rows. Applies field mapping (amount_excl → amount_excl_vat). Computes missing vat from vat_rate (default 15% SA VAT). Computes missing amount_incl_vat = excl + vat. Each row becomes a separate item for row-wise processing.

nodes.name: 🔍 Validate Row — Business Rules
nodes.notes:
Enforces all business rules:
1. Required fields present
2. amount_incl_vat = amount_excl_vat + vat (±0.01 tolerance)
3. VAT rate cross-check vs SA 15% standard
4. Invoice date not in future (Africa/Johannesburg UTC+2)
5. No negative amounts
Returns valid=true/false with detailed validation_notes.

nodes.name: 🔀 Route — Valid vs Invalid
nodes.notes: Splits flow: valid rows go to dedup check → insert. Invalid rows go directly to failure handler.

nodes.name: 🔎 Dedup Check — Query DB
nodes.notes: Checks if (supplier_number, invoice_number) already exists in supplier_invoices. If a row is returned → duplicate. DB enforces UNIQUE constraint as final guard, but we check first to handle gracefully.

nodes.name: 🏷️ Tag — Duplicate or New
nodes.notes: Evaluates the DB query result. If a matching record exists, tags row as duplicate and short-circuits insert. This prevents any duplicate inserts beyond what the DB UNIQUE constraint would catch.

nodes.name: 🔀 Route — Insert or Skip Duplicate
nodes.notes: Final routing gate: only non-duplicate records proceed to INSERT. Duplicates are collected for metrics and email report.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO supplier_invoices (
  invoice_number, supplier_number, supplier_name, department,
  amount_excl_vat, vat, amount_incl_vat, invoice_date,
  source_file_name, source_hash, ingest_timestamp,
  status, validation_notes
) VALUES (
  '{{ $json.invoice_number }}',
  '{{ $json.supplier_number }}',
  '{{ $json.supplier_name }}',
  '{{ $json.department }}',
  {{ $json.amount_excl_vat }},
  {{ $json.vat }},
  {{ $json.amount_incl_vat }},
  '{{ $json.invoice_date }}',
  '{{ $json.sourceFileName }}',
  '{{ $json.sourceHash }}',
  NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Africa/Johannesburg',
  'inserted',
  NULL
)
ON CONFLICT (supplier_number, invoice_number) DO NOTHING

nodes.name: 💾 Insert — supplier_invoices
nodes.notes: Inserts validated, non-duplicate rows into supplier_invoices. Sets status='inserted'. ingest_timestamp uses Africa/Johannesburg timezone. RETURNING clause confirms what was inserted.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO supplier_invoices_failures (
  invoice_number, supplier_number, supplier_name,
  raw_payload, validation_notes,
  source_file_name, source_hash,
  retry_count, created_at
) VALUES (
  {{ $json.invoice_number ? `'${$json.invoice_number}'` : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.supplier_number ? `'${$json.supplier_number}'` : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.supplier_name ? `'${$json.supplier_name}'` : 'NULL' }},
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json).replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::jsonb,
  {{ $json.validation_notes ? `'${$json.validation_notes.replace(/'/g, "''")}'` : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.sourceFileName ? `'${$json.sourceFileName}'` : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.sourceHash ? `'${$json.sourceHash}'` : 'NULL' }},
  0,
  NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Africa/Johannesburg'
)
RETURNING id, invoice_number, validation_notes;

nodes.name: 💾 Insert — supplier_invoices_failures
nodes.notes: Persists failed (invalid validation) rows to the failures table. Stores full raw_payload as JSONB for debugging and potential retry. retry_count starts at 0.

nodes.name: 📌 Mark Duplicate — Collect
nodes.notes: Formats duplicate rows for aggregation into the final email metrics. Duplicates are NOT inserted into the main table.

nodes.name: 📊 Aggregate — Metrics Summary
nodes.notes: Waits for all branches to complete, then aggregates counts: inserted, duplicates, failed. Builds the issue rows array (max 20) for the email alert table.

parameters.jsCode:
// ============================================================ // NODE: Build HTML Email Body // PURPOSE: Generate a professional, color-coded HTML email // summary of the ingest run. // ============================================================ const m = $input.first().json; const { totalProcessed, insertedCount, duplicateCount, failedCount, issueRows, sourceFileName, ingestTime } = m; const executionUrl = `https://johnson-fullstack.app.n8n.cloud/home/workflows`; // Status color coding const overallStatus = failedCount > 0 ? 'COMPLETED WITH ERRORS' : (duplicateCount > 0 ? 'COMPLETED WITH WARNINGS' : 'COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY'); const headerColor = failedCount > 0 ? '#c0392b' : (duplicateCount > 0 ? '#e67e22' : '#27ae60'); // Build issue rows table (only if there are issues) let issueTableHTML = ''; if (issueRows && issueRows.length > 0) { const rowsHTML = issueRows.map(row => { const statusColor = row.status === 'duplicate' ? '#e67e22' : '#c0392b'; const statusBadge = `${row.status.toUpperCase()}`; return ` ${row.invoice_number} ${row.supplier_number} ${row.supplier_name} ${statusBadge} ${row.reason} `; }).join(''); issueTableHTML = `

⚠️ Issues Requiring Attention (${issueRows.length})

${rowsHTML}
Invoice # Supplier # Supplier Name Status Reason
`; } const emailHTML = `

📄 Supplier Invoice Ingest Report

${overallStatus}

📁 Source File ${sourceFileName} 🕐 Ingest Time ${ingestTime}

📊 Run Summary

${totalProcessed}
TOTAL PROCESSED
${insertedCount}
✅ INSERTED
${duplicateCount}
⚠️ DUPLICATES
${failedCount}
❌ FAILED
${issueTableHTML || '

✅ All invoices processed successfully with no issues.

'}

Generated by Supplier Invoice Ingest Pipeline · n8n Automation · Africa/Johannesburg

View Execution in n8n

` const subject = `Supplier Ingest: ${insertedCount} ok, ${duplicateCount} dup, ${failedCount} failed — ${sourceFileName}`; return [{ json: { emailSubject: subject, emailHTML, ...m } }];

nodes.name: ✉️ Build — HTML Email Body
nodes.notes: Generates a professional HTML email with: color-coded header (green/orange/red based on results), metric summary cards, and a detailed issues table. Subject line follows the required format.

nodes.name: 📧 Send — Ingest Alert Email
nodes.notes: Sends the HTML ingest summary email. Configure: (1) Set your recipient email address. (2) Connect Gmail OAuth2 credential. Subject dynamically shows counts. HTML body is color-coded with metrics cards and issue table.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO supplier_invoices_staging (
  invoice_number, supplier_number, supplier_name, department,
  amount_excl_vat, vat, amount_incl_vat, invoice_date,
  source_file_name, source_hash, ingest_timestamp,
  status, validation_notes
) VALUES (
  '{{ $json.invoice_number }}',
  '{{ $json.supplier_number }}',
  '{{ $json.supplier_name }}',
  '{{ $json.department }}',
  {{ $json.amount_excl_vat }},
  {{ $json.vat }},
  {{ $json.amount_incl_vat }},
  '{{ $json.invoice_date }}',
  '{{ $json.sourceFileName }}',
  '{{ $json.sourceHash }}',
  NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Africa/Johannesburg',
  'inserted',
  NULL
)
ON CONFLICT (supplier_number, invoice_number) DO NOTHING

nodes.name: 💾 Insert — supplier_invoices_staging
nodes.notes: Inserts validated, non-duplicate rows into supplier_invoices. Sets status='inserted'. ingest_timestamp uses Africa/Johannesburg timezone. RETURNING clause confirms what was inserted.

parameters.content:
📥 TRIGGER SOURCES
Two input sources supported:
1. Webhook — HTTP multipart/form-data file upload
2. Google Drive — watches folder, auto-triggers on new CSV file
Both paths merge at Config and follow identical pipeline.

⚙️ PIPELINE CONFIG
DRY_RUN flag controls routing:
- false → inserts into supplier_invoices (live)
- true  → inserts into supplier_invoices_staging (safe testing)
Change this flag before running to switch modes.

🔐 FILE PROCESSING
1. SHA-256 hash computed for idempotency (stored as source_hash)
2. Source file name extracted from binary metadata
3. Binary field renamed to 'file' regardless of source
   (Webhook = 'file', Google Drive = 'data') 
   so Extract from File always works correctly

✅ VALIDATION RULES (5 rules)
1. Required fields — invoice_number, supplier_number, supplier_name, department, invoice_date, amounts
2. Math check — amount_incl_vat = amount_excl_vat + vat (±0.01 tolerance)
3. VAT rate — derived rate must equal 15% (SA), mismatch = WARNING not error
4. Future date — invoice_date must not be in the future (Africa/Johannesburg timezone)
5. Negative amounts — all amounts must be >= 0

Errors → supplier_invoices_failures
Warnings → proceed with note in validation_notes

🔎 DEDUPLICATION (3 levels)
1. Within-batch — JavaScript Set tracks seen (supplier_number, invoice_number) 
   pairs in current CSV. First occurrence wins, rest marked duplicate.
2. Cross-run — DB query checks supplier_invoices before each insert.
3. Database constraint — UNIQUE (supplier_number, invoice_number) 
   + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING as final safety net.

💾 INSERT ROUTING
DRY_RUN: false → supplier_invoices (live)
DRY_RUN: true  → supplier_invoices_staging (staging)

ON CONFLICT (supplier_number, invoice_number) DO NOTHING
ensures no crashes on edge cases.

Duplicates → Mark Duplicate — Collect (skipped, counted only)
Failures   → supplier_invoices_failures (with retry_count = 0)

📧 EMAIL ALERT
Aggregates metrics from all 4 branches:
- Inserted (from live/staging insert)
- Duplicates (from Mark Duplicate — Collect)
- Failed (from supplier_invoices_failures)

Subject: Supplier Ingest: {inserted} ok, {duplicates} dup, {failed} failed — {filename}
Header colour: Green (success) / Orange (duplicates) / Red (failures)
Body: metric cards + issues table (max 20 rows)
Single email per run guaranteed by Aggregate guard.

main.node: 📋 Parse CSV — Map Fields + Fill Math
